text: Not on the current corpus. Variant B (a section-predictor that emits the subgoal frontier + a solver) has essentially no within-corpus training signal: of the 607 verified (rough, fine) pairs, 93.1% (565) are flat with ZERO named have-nodes, only 42 (6.9%) expose any named frontier and just 27 (4.4%) expose a non-trivial >=2-subgoal frontier (among those 42, median 2 / max 7 have-nodes). ~27 supervised frontiers is far too few to SFT a section-predictor, so the 'fewer LLM calls' planner cannot even be trained, let alone beat a single-stage baseline. This is the same ~4% structural ceiling found in e-0004 (decomposability) and the supply problem behind q-0008 (length confound). q-0009 is therefore blocked on the identical unblock: re-extracting proofs with a shrunk expansion frontier (F_size, which e-0004 showed raises decomposable counts) -- a real Modal pipeline change, not a cheap post-processing step. Untested empirically (no model trained); this is a data-availability gate, not an outcome on pass@k.
